Mix the crumbs with apricot jam and generous amounts of rum until you get a moist, moldable mixture.
Line a small square mold or cut out rectangles. Layer: sponge base, rum-soaked crumb mixture, sponge top.
Brush all sides with warm apricot jam (Aprikotur) for a smooth finish.
Prepare the pink fondant: mix powdered sugar with lemon juice and a drop of pink food coloring until glossy. Dip the top of each Krapfen.
Melt dark chocolate and dip the bottom of each Krapfen.
Let set completely on a wire rack. The finished Punschkrapfen should have a pink top, chocolate bottom, and be perfectly square.

gugelhupf.title: Gugelhupf
gugelhupf.description: The classic Austrian marble Bundt cake with its iconic fluted ring shape — a beloved Sunday afternoon coffee cake with swirls of vanilla and chocolate.
steps: Preheat oven to 170°C (340°F). Generously butter and flour a Gugelhupf (Bundt) mold.
Beat the room-temperature butter with sugar and vanilla sugar until very light and fluffy — at least 5 minutes.
Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Mix flour with baking powder. Alternately fold in the flour mixture and milk in three additions.
Divide the batter in half. Mix one half with cocoa and rum.
Spoon alternating layers of light and dark batter into the mold. Use a knife to create a marble swirl pattern.
Bake for 45-50 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Let cool in the mold for 15 minutes, then invert. Dust generously with powdered sugar.

bauernbrot.title: Bauernbrot
bauernbrot.description: Rustic Austrian farmhouse bread with a thick, crackled crust and a dense, flavorful crumb — made with a mix of rye and wheat flour and real sourdough.
steps: Mix the sourdough starter with the flours, water, salt, caraway seeds, and bread spice. Knead for about 10 minutes until smooth.
Cover and let the dough rise at room temperature for at least 2 hours (or overnight in the fridge for more flavor).
Shape the dough into a round loaf on a floured surface. Place on a baking sheet or in a proofing basket.
Let proof for another 45 minutes. Preheat the oven to 240°C (465°F) with a pan of water on the bottom rack for steam.
Score the top of the loaf with a sharp knife or razor blade.
Bake for 15 minutes at 240°C, then reduce to 200°C (390°F) and bake for another 35 minutes until the crust is deep brown.
The bread is done when it sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom. Let cool completely on a wire rack before slicing.

schwarzbrot.title: Schwarzbrot
schwarzbrot.description: Dense, dark Austrian rye bread — slow-baked with a rich, slightly sweet flavor from molasses. A traditional bread that pairs perfectly with butter, cold cuts, and cheese.
steps: Combine the sourdough starter with rye flour, whole wheat flour, water, salt, molasses, caraway, and coriander. Mix well.
Knead for 8-10 minutes. Rye dough will remain stickier than wheat dough — this is normal.
Place in a greased loaf pan. Smooth the top with wet hands. Cover and let rise for 1-2 hours.
Preheat oven to 220°C (430°F). Brush the top with water.
Bake for 15 minutes at 220°C, then reduce to 180°C (350°F) and bake for another 45 minutes.
Let cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack. Wait at least 24 hours before slicing for the best flavor and texture.

kornspitz.title: Kornspitz
kornspitz.description: Crunchy Austrian multigrain bread rolls with pointed ends, covered in a mix of seeds — a wholesome, nutty-flavored roll that's a staple at every Austrian bakery.
steps: Dissolve the yeast in lukewarm water. Combine all three flours with salt.
Add the yeast water and knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Fold in half of the seeds. Cover and let rise for 1 hour.
Divide into 8 pieces. Shape each into a diamond/elongated roll with pointed ends.
Brush with water and roll in the remaining seeds. Place on a lined baking sheet.
Let rise for 30 minutes. Bake at 220°C (430°F) for 18-20 minutes until dark and crusty.

sonnenblumenkernenweckerl.title: Sonnenblumenkernenweckerl
sonnenblumenkernenweckerl.description: Soft Austrian bread rolls generously coated with sunflower seeds — golden-crusted on the outside, fluffy white on the inside. A breakfast favorite.
steps: Dissolve the yeast in lukewarm water with the sugar. Mix flour, salt, and softened butter.
Add the yeast mixture and knead for 10 minutes until smooth.
Fold in half the sunflower seeds. Cover and let rise for 1 hour.
Divide into 8 pieces and shape into round rolls. Brush with water and press the tops into the remaining sunflower seeds.
Place seed-side up on a lined baking sheet. Let rise 30 minutes. Bake at 220°C (430°F) for 18-20 minutes until golden.

laugenstangerl.title: Laugenstangerl
laugenstangerl.description: Austrian pretzel sticks with a glossy, dark mahogany crust and fluffy interior — dipped in lye solution for that distinctive pretzel flavor and topped with coarse salt.
steps: Dissolve the yeast in lukewarm water with sugar. Mix flour and salt, add yeast mixture and softened butter.
Knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover and let rise for 1 hour.
Divide into 8 equal pieces. Roll each into a stick about 20 cm long.
Prepare the lye bath: dissolve baking soda in 1 liter of boiling water. Let cool slightly.
Briefly dip each stick into the lye bath (about 30 seconds). Place on a lined baking sheet.
Score each Stangerl across the top with a sharp knife. Sprinkle with coarse salt.
Bake at 220°C (430°F) for 12-15 minutes until deep brown and glossy.

currywurst.title: Currywurst
currywurst.description: A beloved street food classic — grilled bratwurst sliced and smothered in a tangy curry ketchup sauce, served with crispy French fries.
steps: Finely dice the onion. Heat oil in a small saucepan and sauté the onion until translucent.
Add ketchup, half the curry powder, paprika, Worcestershire sauce, and sugar. Simmer the curry sauce for 10 minutes on low heat.
Pan-fry or grill the bratwurst until cooked through and golden brown on all sides.
Slice the cooked bratwurst into bite-sized pieces. Arrange in a serving tray.
Pour the warm curry sauce generously over the sliced sausage. Sprinkle with remaining curry powder. Serve with French fries.

frankfurter.title: Frankfurter Würstel
frankfurter.description: Classic Austrian boiled pork sausages served simply with sweet mustard, freshly grated horseradish, and a Semmel — the quintessential Würstelstand experience.
steps: Heat water in a pot until just below boiling (about 75°C/167°F). Never boil Frankfurter — they will burst and lose their snap.
Place the sausages in the hot water and let them warm through for 8-10 minutes.
Serve immediately on a plate with a generous dollop of sweet mustard, freshly grated horseradish, and a fresh Semmel on the side.
